sense.definition: a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug that helps with pain, swelling, and fever

examples.x: I take ibuprofen when my head hurts.
She uses ibuprofen for her sore muscles after exercising.
Ibuprofen helps to ease the pain of menstrual cramps.
Ibuprofen is available over-the-counter at most pharmacies.
My mom always keeps ibuprofen in the medicine cabinet for emergencies.

sense.definition: a form of medicine mainly used to control mood in individuals with bipolar disorder

examples.x: Some people may experience side effects when starting lithium treatment.
She takes lithium every day to help manage her bipolar disorder.
Individuals on lithium should stay hydrated, as dehydration can affect its effectiveness.
Long-term use of lithium may require kidney function tests to monitor potential adverse effects.
The psychiatrist adjusted the dosage of lithium to manage the patient's bipolar symptoms.

sense.definition: a narcotic drug that is extracted from opium and is medicinally used to relieve pain
an analgesic drug that alleviates mild or chronic pain and reduces fever in most cases
any of variation of antibiotics obtained from Penicillium moulds and used to treat or prevent a wide range of bacterial infections

examples.x: He is allergic to penicillin and needs an alternative antibiotic.
The discovery of penicillin revolutionized medicine.
The patient responded well to the penicillin treatment.

sense.definition: a tranquilizing drug, often used as a muscle relaxant, that can relieve anxiety and tension
a medicine that helps men with difficulties in getting or maintaining an erection

examples.x: Some men use Viagra to enhance their sexual performance.
John noticed a positive difference after taking Viagra.
The effects of Viagra typically last for a few hours.
The medication's active ingredient in Viagra is sildenafil.
It's important not to take more than the recommended dose of Viagra.

sense.definition: a milky white liquid medication containing magnesium hydroxide that helps with constipation and stomach discomfort

examples.x: She took a spoonful of milk of magnesia to relieve her upset stomach.
The pharmacist recommended milk of magnesia for my indigestion.
Milk of magnesia is available over-the-counter at most pharmacies.
My mom always keeps a bottle of milk of magnesia in the medicine cabinet.
Some people prefer the mint-flavored milk of magnesia for a more pleasant taste.

sense.definition: a synthetic compound used to treat the illness of depression, panic attacks, certain eating disorders, and obsessive-compulsive disorder
a powerful sedative primarily used to treat severe insomnia or induce anesthesia, commonly misused for its ability to cause memory loss and sedation

examples.x: She felt unwell after suspecting someone had slipped Rohypnol into her drink.
The club implemented measures to prevent the presence of Rohypnol.
The police issued a warning about the illegal use of Rohypnol.
Students were educated about the risks of Rohypnol during a safety workshop.
Rohypnol is illegal in many countries due to its association with drug-facilitated crimes.

sense.definition: a medicine that fights bacteria, commonly used to treat tuberculosis and other bacterial infections

examples.x: The doctor prescribed streptomycin to cure my ear infection.
Jay was feeling much better after taking the streptomycin for his throat infection.
After taking streptomycin, the patient's symptoms improved rapidly.
The child's fever went down after a few days of streptomycin treatment.
Doctors often use streptomycin to treat serious bacterial diseases.

sense.definition: a medicine that helps treat breast cancer by blocking the effects of estrogen on cancer cells

examples.x: Emily takes tamoxifen every day to help her fight breast cancer.
The doctor prescribed tamoxifen as part of Jane's cancer treatment plan.
The nurse provided a schedule for taking tamoxifen along with other medications.

sense.definition: a brand of acetaminophen, a widely used over-the-counter pain reliever and fever reducer

examples.x: When I had a cold, I relied on Tylenol to ease my discomfort.
Tylenol is one of the go-to choices for pain relief.
After her headache, Lisa took Tylenol for relief.
Taking Tylenol with food can help prevent stomach upset.
Tylenol is often used for reducing fever in children.

sense.definition: a medicine that helps prevent blood clots from forming in the body

examples.x: Tim's grandmother uses a pill organizer for her daily warfarin.
Jane's physician adjusted her warfarin dosage for optimal effect.
Warfarin helps thin the blood and reduces the risk of clotting.
Warfarin is often prescribed after certain heart surgeries.
The doctor explained the potential side effects of warfarin to the patient.

sense.definition: a medicine that helps lower cholesterol and prevents heart problems

examples.x: Sam's doctor prescribed atorvastatin to control his cholesterol levels.
Taking atorvastatin regularly can help prevent heart issues.
Mary uses atorvastatin to manage her high cholesterol.

sense.definition: a synthetic thyroid hormone used to treat hypothyroidism and other thyroid-related conditions
a medicine that helps with stomach problems by lowering the amount of acid it produces

examples.x: Jenny takes omeprazole to ease her acid reflux symptoms.
Bob's pharmacist explained how to take omeprazole for best results.
The doctor recommended omeprazole for Tom's stomach ulcer.
The nurse provided instructions on taking omeprazole with or without food.

sense.definition: a type of medicine used to treat allergies or neutralize their effects

examples.x: He took an antihistamine to relieve his allergy symptoms.
Antihistamines can help reduce sneezing and itching caused by allergies.
The doctor recommended an antihistamine for his seasonal allergies.
She felt better after taking an antihistamine for her hay fever.
He was advised to take an antihistamine before traveling to avoid allergic reactions.

sense.definition: a drug that is used to destroy bacteria or stop their growth, like Penicillin
a medication commonly prescribed to treat nerve pain and seizures by stabilizing electrical activity in the brain

examples.x: Emily takes gabapentin to help with her nerve pain.
Tim's dosage of gabapentin was adjusted for better pain relief.
The pharmacist explained how to take gabapentin for optimal results.

sense.definition: a medicine that helps people breathe better by relaxing the muscles in their airways

examples.x: Emily uses albuterol when she has trouble breathing due to asthma.
Albuterol helps open up the airways for easier breathing.
He keeps albuterol in his inhaler for emergency use.
Jane carries albuterol with her in case of respiratory distress.
Albuterol provides relief by relaxing the muscles around the airways during an asthma attack.

sense.definition: a medication used to treat high blood pressure, heart conditions, and kidney damage in diabetes
an oral medication that helps control blood sugar in people with type 2 diabetes

examples.x: Metformin is one of the first-line treatments for type 2 diabetes.
The nurse provided instructions on when and how to take metformin.
Daniel takes metformin every day to help manage his diabetes.
Metformin helps the body use insulin more effectively.
Mary's pharmacist explained how metformin works to regulate blood sugar.

sense.definition: a prescription opioid for moderate to severe pain, with potential for dependence
a medicine used to help people with anxiety and panic attacks feel calmer

examples.x: Sarah takes alprazolam when she feels very anxious.
The doctor prescribed alprazolam to help with Alex's panic attacks.
The nurse provided instructions on when and how to take alprazolam.

sense.definition: a medicine that helps slow down the HIV virus in people with AIDS

examples.x: Lisa takes zidovudine to manage her HIV infection.
Zidovudine helps slow down the progression of the HIV virus.
Tim's doctor adjusted his zidovudine dosage for better virus control.
Zidovudine is crucial in managing the HIV virus and preventing complications.

sense.definition: a type of medicine taken to relieve pain, bring down a fever, etc.

examples.x: She took two aspirin tablets to relieve her headache.
The doctor recommended taking aspirin to reduce the fever.
He keeps a bottle of aspirin in his desk drawer for emergencies.
After spraining his ankle, he took an aspirin to help with the pain.
Aspirin is often used to alleviate the symptoms of the common cold.

sense.definition: a sleep-inducing drug used to treat or reduce mild to moderate pain
a combination of medications, usually mifepristone followed by misoprostol, used to terminate an early pregnancy

examples.x: Mary appreciated the privacy and control that the abortion pill provided.
The doctor explained the process of taking the abortion pill to Emily.
The abortion pill is a safe and legal way to end an early pregnancy.
The abortion pill is typically taken in two steps for maximum effectiveness.
Sarah chose the abortion pill as her preferred method for terminating the pregnancy.

sense.definition: a medicine taken by mouth to prevent pregnancy by stopping the release of eggs and making it harder for sperm to reach the egg

examples.x: Lisa takes a birth control pill every day to avoid getting pregnant.
Taking birth control pills can help with acne and menstrual cramps for some women.
John's partner chose birth control pills as a convenient family planning option.
Birth control pills are available by prescription and should be taken as directed.
John's partner chose birth control pills as a convenient family planning option.
